cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a donut and coffee from this highly loved vegan cafe on Santa Clara St — the donuts are the real draw, so grab a couple and take your time.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hit it early before the good stuff sells out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition on your way through Santa Clara — it's a visually interesting show worth at least an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, and daytime-only so now is the time to catch it.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and just pick whatever looks good — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof and you can grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown with no wrong answer on what to order.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre for the SJSU Opera Theatre double bill — La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator are two very different pieces and together they make for a genuinely interesting afternoon of live performance.
cards.why: It's free and it's live opera in a great venue, which is a rare Sunday afternoon treat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge, where you pick a piece, paint it however you want, and they fire it for you — genuinely fun for adults and a great low-key way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Wander through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of visual art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely interesting way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history and culture of martial arts in America.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and the kind of thing you'd never stumble on otherwise.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, ramen, banh mi, whatever sounds good. Snag a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch downtown when you can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at the SJSU Student Union Bowling Center. It's a real lanes setup, not a pop-up, and it's open to the public.
cards.why: Low-key competitive and way more fun than it sounds on a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row in the early evening, browse the boutiques, and find a table at one of the outdoor restaurants when you're ready to sit down.
cards.why: The boulevard is relaxed on a weekday evening and the patio vibes are solid.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - San Jose
cards.address: 1495 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at LUNA Mexican Kitchen on The Alameda. Order margaritas and something off the dinner menu and call it a good Monday.
cards.why: Solid Mexican food with a vibe that doesn't feel rushed.

cards.name: The Midwife and The Baker
cards.address: 846 Independence Ave, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Midwife and the Baker is a Mountain View morning institution — grab a pastry and coffee and ease into the day.
cards.why: Hard to beat a bakery this good on a cool morning.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Rancho San Antonio — the loop out to the farm overlook is a solid two hours without being brutal.
cards.why: Cool temps make this the perfect window for a hike before it gets busy.

cards.name: Volunteering at Stanford Educational Farm - Weekday
cards.address: 175 Electioneer Rd, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours volunteering at Stanford's Educational Farm, working in the fields alongside others on a weekday morning session.
cards.why: It's a genuinely satisfying way to spend time outside and give back.

cards.name: Katsu
cards.address: 859 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Katsu in Mountain View has an absurdly good reputation for a reason — try the local favorite and don't skip the sides.
cards.why: You'll want a real lunch after a morning of physical activity.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Erika Chong Shuch's exhibition at Stanford is an immersive new work worth exploring slowly — give yourself time to sit with it.
cards.why: Free contemporary art at Stanford is always worth the stop.

cards.name: Arisa Thai Massage
cards.address: 4546 El Camino Real Suite 203, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Arisa Thai Massage in Los Altos and let someone sort out whatever the hike did to your legs.
cards.why: After a full morning outside, this is exactly what the afternoon calls for.

cards.name: Laasya Art Gallery
cards.address: 2225 E Bayshore Rd #100-7127, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening browsing Laasya Art Gallery in Palo Alto, which carries eclectic work worth more than a quick glance.
cards.why: A relaxed gallery visit is a perfect low-key cap to an active day.

cards.name: Kovai Cafe - Milpitas
cards.address: 181 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Kovai Cafe, a beloved South Indian spot in Milpitas — grab a dosa or some idli and a strong coffee to get the day going.
cards.why: It opens early and the food is genuinely excellent for a Sunday morning.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Alum Rock Park and walk the creek trails through the foothills. It's a real hike with some payoff views, not just a flat loop.
cards.why: Low 50s at 9 AM warming up to 75 makes this the sweet spot for a morning hike.

cards.name: The Orange Tabby
cards.address: 1344 Ridder Park Dr, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: The Orange Tabby is a cafe with a cult following. Grab lunch and linger a bit — the vibe is cozy and the food hits.
cards.why: It closes at 4 PM so Sunday lunch is the move.

cards.name: EDscapade Games
cards.address: 1150 Murphy Ave #200, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: EDscapade Games has some of the most creative escape rooms in the area. Book a room and see if you and your crew can actually solve it.
cards.why: Perfect afternoon activity when you want something that actually requires brainpower.

cards.name: Perspire Sauna Studio
cards.address: 3935 Rivermark Plaza, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Perspire Sauna Studio in Santa Clara. Infrared sauna sessions are a solid way to reset after a full morning of activity.
cards.why: Your legs will thank you after the hike and you'll feel completely fresh for the evening.

cards.name: Ichika
cards.address: 1728 N Milpitas Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Ichika opens at 5 PM and it's worth going a little early to beat the crowd. Order whatever looks good off the menu and take your time.
cards.why: It's one of the better dinner options in Milpitas and the price is a steal for the quality.

cards.name: Brew Bytes Cafe
cards.address: 19998 Homestead Rd Suite C, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a solid espresso or latte at this cozy Cupertino cafe — grab a seat, wake up slowly, and fuel up before the day starts.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a Sunday with good coffee and no rush.

cards.name: kawaii diy studio - DIY Decoden Art Studio
cards.address: 19701 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into this DIY decoden art studio and make something weird and fun — bedazzle a phone case, a mirror, a trinket box, whatever calls to you.
cards.why: It's a surprisingly absorbing way to spend a Sunday morning and you leave with something to show for it.

cards.name: Bookasaurus
cards.address: 125 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through Bookasaurus just up the street and browse their shelves — it's the kind of used bookstore where you always find something you weren't looking for.
cards.why: A great post-lunch slow-down before the afternoon picks up again.

cards.name: Schaefer Park
cards.address: 914 Mountain View Ave, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Schaefer Park and take a walk while the afternoon warms up. It's a genuinely pleasant green space good for stretching out after lunch.
cards.why: 75 degrees and sunny on a Sunday afternoon is exactly what a park was made for.

cards.name: Watercourse Way Bath House Spa
cards.address: 165 Channing Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Book a private soaking tub or hot tub session at Watercourse Way and spend a couple hours fully decompressing. It's a proper bathhouse experience, not a spa chain.
cards.why: The perfect way to cap off the active part of the day before dinner.

cards.why: Sometimes the food court spot is genuinely the best call, and this one delivers.

cards.name: Basquia Bakery
cards.address: 1775 E Bayshore Rd, East Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Basquia Bakery with a pastry and coffee to ease into the day.
cards.why: A beloved local bakery is the right way to kick off a Sunday.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hike the Dish Trail loop through the Stanford foothills — the views open up as you climb and the rolling hills look great on a crisp spring morning.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a walk that actually has a payoff at the top.

cards.name: Highlights Tour | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Head over to the Anderson Collection on Stanford's campus for the Highlights Tour — it's a focused walk through some genuinely impressive American modern and contemporary art.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a Sunday afternoon is hard to beat.

cards.name: Bell's Books
cards.address: 536 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Bell's Books on Emerson Street, a proper used and antiquarian bookshop where you can easily lose an hour flipping through unexpected finds.
cards.why: A rainy-or-sunny afternoon at a great indie bookstore never feels like a waste of time.

cards.name: K Spa and Bodywork
cards.address: 151 2nd St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage or bodywork session at K Spa in Los Altos, a solid spot to decompress after a full morning of hiking and an afternoon on your feet.
cards.why: You've earned it after that trail.
